perturbation.original_text: 3.1 This Agreement shall be taken to have commenced , notwithstanding the signature date hereof , on the earliest of: 3.1.1 the date of signature of this agreement by the Buyer; or 3.1.2 the date of the first meeting or correspondence between Aldes and the Buyer regarding any Business/es and/or Seller/s ; and shall continue in perpetuity thereafter .
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 This Agreement shall be taken to have commenced , notwithstanding the signature date hereof , on the earliest of: 3.1.1 the date of signature of this agreement by the Buyer; or 3.1.2 the date of the first meeting or correspondence between Aldes and the Buyer regarding any Business/es and/or Seller/s ; and shall continue for a period of 1 year, with automatic renewal for subsequent 1-year periods unless either party provides written notice of termination 30 days prior to the renewal date. However, in some cases, the agreement may continue in perpetuity thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity in the duration of the agreement. Initially, the agreement is stated to continue 'in perpetuity'. The added text introduces a one-year term with automatic renewal, contradicting the 'in perpetuity' clause, create an in-text contradiction. This affects the enforceability of obligations under the agreement, as it's unclear whether the agreement truly lasts forever or is subject to periodic renewal and potential termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 This Agreement shall be taken to have commenced , notwithstanding the signature date hereof , on the earliest of: 3.1.1 the date of signature of this agreement by the Buyer; or 3.1.2 the date of the first meeting or correspondence between Aldes and the Buyer regarding any Business/es and/or Seller/s ; and shall continue for a period of 1 year, with automatic renewal for subsequent 1-year periods unless either party provides written notice of termination 30 days prior to the renewal date. However, in some cases, the agreement may continue in perpetuity thereafter.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement continues 'in perpetuity'. The changed text introduces a one-year term with automatic renewal but includes a caveat that 'in some cases, the agreement may continue in perpetuity thereafter'. This contradicts the primary term of one year with automatic renewal, as perpetuity implies an indefinite duration without renewal or termination possibilities, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.2 The Buyer warrants and guarantees that it will never circumvent Aldes (albeit directly itself or indirectly through any of its subsidiaries, holding companies, group companies, related parties and/or inter -related parties or otherwise indirectly ), in negot iating or concluding any transactions , arrangements and/or agreements contemplated in clauses 2.1 and/or 2.2 above with (or with regard to) any Seller/s and/or Business /es. For avoidance of doubt, the Buyer will at all times act in good faith and will only transact and deal with Seller/s and/or Business/es, via the brokerage , intervention and mediation of Aldes.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2 The Buyer warrants and guarantees that it will never intentionally circumvent Aldes (albeit directly itself or indirectly through any of its subsidiaries, holding companies, group companies, related parties and/or inter -related parties or otherwise indirectly ), in negotiating or concluding any transactions , arrangements and/or agreements contemplated in clauses 2.1 and/or 2.2 above with (or with regard to) any Seller/s and/or Business /es. For avoidance of doubt, the Buyer will at all times act in good faith and will only transact and deal with Seller/s and/or Business/es, via the brokerage , intervention and mediation of Aldes, unless opportunities arise independently.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'intentionally' weakens the non-circumvention clause. The phrase 'unless opportunities arise independently' creates an exception, contradicting the original clause's strict prohibition. This introduces an ambiguity as the contract now allows room for interpretations of what constitutes 'independent opportunities,' potentially leading to disputes over whether the Buyer circumvented Aldes or legitimately pursued an independent opportunity. Also, it is contradicting with the phrase 'For avoidance of doubt, the Buyer will at all times act in good faith and will only transact and deal with Seller/s and/or Business/es, via the brokerage , intervention and mediation of Aldes.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 The Buyer warrants and guarantees that it will never intentionally circumvent Aldes (albeit directly itself or indirectly through any of its subsidiaries, holding companies, group companies, related parties and/or inter -related parties or otherwise indirectly ), in negotiating or concluding any transactions , arrangements and/or agreements contemplated in clauses 2.1 and/or 2.2 above with (or with regard to) any Seller/s and/or Business /es. For avoidance of doubt, the Buyer will at all times act in good faith and will only transact and deal with Seller/s and/or Business/es, via the brokerage , intervention and mediation of Aldes, unless opportunities arise independently.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'intentionally' and 'unless opportunities arise independently' to the original non-circumvention clause. This directly contradicts the part of the clause that states 'For avoidance of doubt, the Buyer will at all times act in good faith and will only transact and deal with Seller/s and/or Business/es, via the brokerage , intervention and mediation of Aldes.' The addition of the exception allows for independent dealings, creating a clear contradiction with the original intention to exclusively use Aldes' services.

perturbation.original_text: 7.1 No alteration or variation to or of this Agreement shall be of any force or effect unless it is recorded in writing and signed by the Buyer and Aldes .
perturbation.changed_text: 7.1 No alteration or variation to or of this Agreement shall be of any force or effect unless it is recorded in writing and signed by the Buyer and Aldes, except for minor clarifications which may be agreed upon verbally.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an exception to the requirement for written modifications. The addition of 'except for minor clarifications which may be agreed upon verbally' creates ambiguity and contradicts the strict requirement for written agreements. It becomes unclear what constitutes a 'minor clarification' and could lead to disputes over the enforceability of verbal agreements that deviate from the written contract, leading to a In-Text contradiction. The original phrase is being contradicted by the added phrase.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 No alteration or variation to or of this Agreement shall be of any force or effect unless it is recorded in writing and signed by the Buyer and Aldes, except for minor clarifications which may be agreed upon verbally.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception ('except for minor clarifications which may be agreed upon verbally') to the original strict requirement that all alterations or variations must be in writing and signed. This creates a contradiction because the original text states that 'No alteration or variation... shall be of any force or effect unless it is recorded in writing'. The addition allows for verbal agreements, directly contradicting the prior statement. The contradicted text is the same section as the changed text, and the explanation specifically indicates how the added phrase creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 The Buyer notes that Aldes’ decision to introduce any Business/es for sale to the Buyer (and the Seller/s and/or Business/es decision to enter into discussions with the Buyer) is, in part, based on the information presented by the Buyer under and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile on the face of this Agreement. As such, the Buyer warrants and guarantees that all information presented and p rovided via and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile was, is and will remain true, complete and accurate and that it will immediately inform Aldes should there be any change in t he information provided/presented.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 The Buyer notes that Aldes’ decision to introduce any Business/es for sale to the Buyer (and the Seller/s and/or Business/es decision to enter into discussions with the Buyer) is, in part, based on the information presented by the Buyer under and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile on the face of this Agreement. However, Aldes can still propose to the buyer, even if there's misleading informartion. As such, the Buyer warrants and guarantees that all information presented and p rovided via and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile was, is and will remain true, complete and accurate and that it will immediately inform Aldes should there be any change in t he information provided/presented.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an ambiguity regarding the importance of the Buyer’s Profile information. Initially, the profile is presented as a key factor in Aldes' decision to introduce Business/es. The added text introduces a contradiction since is now mentioning that Aldes can still propose, even if the information is misleading. This creates uncertainty and contradiction regarding the reliability of the initial agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 The Buyer notes that Aldes’ decision to introduce any Business/es for sale to the Buyer (and the Seller/s and/or Business/es decision to enter into discussions with the Buyer) is, in part, based on the information presented by the Buyer under and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile on the face of this Agreement. However, Aldes can still propose to the buyer, even if there's misleading informartion. As such, the Buyer warrants and guarantees that all information presented and p rovided via and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile was, is and will remain true, complete and accurate and that it will immediately inform Aldes should there be any change in t he information provided/presented.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "However, Aldes can still propose to the buyer, even if there's misleading informartion." to section 8.1. The original text states that Aldes' decision to introduce businesses for sale to the buyer is based on the information presented in the Buyer's Profile, implying that accurate information is important for Aldes' decision-making. The added text directly contradicts this by stating that Aldes can still propose even if there's misleading information. This creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding the importance and reliability of the information provided in the Buyer's Profile, thus creating an ambiguity and uncertainty on the terms of the contract.
